questions.question: Which of the following is required for two methods to be considered overloaded?
options: They must have different return types
They must have different parameter lists
They must have different parameter names
They must be in different classes
questions.answer: They must have different parameter lists

questions.question: Which change alone does NOT create a valid method overload?
options: Changing the order of parameter types
Compilation error — no int version exists
The int 5 is promoted to double 5.0 and the method executes
questions.answer: The int 5 is promoted to double 5.0 and the method executes

questions.question: Which of the following pairs of methods will cause a compilation error if placed in the same class?
options: void show(int x) and void show(double x)
void show(int x) and void show(int x, int y)
void show(String s) and void show(int x)
Compilation error — char is not int or double

questions.question: How does method overloading relate to constructor overloading?
options: Constructor overloading follows the same rules — same name, different parameter lists
Only constructors can be overloaded, not regular methods
Constructors must have different return types to be overloaded
questions.answer: Constructor overloading follows the same rules — same name, different parameter lists

questions.question: Which Java class is a well-known example of heavy method overloading?
options: Math — the random() method has 10 overloads
PrintStream — println() accepts String, int, double, boolean, etc.
Scanner — nextInt() has overloads for every type
String — the length() method has 5 overloads
questions.answer: PrintStream — println() accepts String, int, double, boolean, etc.

questions.question: What is printed when process(Integer.valueOf(5)) is called, given the same two methods from the previous question?
In a static method, which of the following can you directly access without an object reference?

options: It compiles and sets the width to 20
Runtime error — cannot access private fields
It compiles but the value is not actually changed

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'this' keyword in the setter: public void setWidth(int width) { this.width = width; }?
options: It creates a new instance of the class
It distinguishes the instance variable from the parameter with the same name
It is required syntax for all setters in Java
questions.answer: It distinguishes the instance variable from the parameter with the same name

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid set of overloaded methods?
options: void run(int x) and int run(int x)
void run(int x) and void run(int y)
void run(int x) and void run(double x)
void run(int x) and void run(int x)
questions.answer: void run(int x) and void run(double x)

questions.question: When designing overloaded formatCurrency methods, which set of signatures is a valid overloading chain?
options: formatCurrency(double), formatCurrency(double, String), formatCurrency(double, String, int)
formatCurrency(double amount), formatCurrency(double value), formatCurrency(double price)
String formatCurrency(double), int formatCurrency(double), void formatCurrency(double)
questions.answer: formatCurrency(double), formatCurrency(double, String), formatCurrency(double, String, int)

questions.question:
Given overloaded repeat methods:
String repeat(String text, int times)
String repeat(char c, int times)
String repeat(String text, int times, String separator)
Which method is called by repeat('*', 5)?
options: repeat(String text, int times) because char is promoted to String
repeat(String text, int times, String separator) with default separator
Compilation error — char cannot match any overload

questions.question:
Given overloaded max methods:
int max(int a, int b)
double max(double a, double b)
int max(int a, int b, int c)
int max(int[] arr)
What is the return type of max(3.14, 2.71)?
options: Compilation error — doubles cannot be compared

questions.question: Why should a utility class like Formatter include a private constructor?
options: To allow subclasses to override its methods
To prevent instantiation since all methods are static
questions.answer: To prevent instantiation since all methods are static

questions.question: In the Student class capstone, which design principle is demonstrated by making fields private and providing getters and setters?
In the capstone Student class, the studentId field is auto-assigned using a static counter. What does this static counter ensure?
options: Each student gets a random unique ID
Each student gets a sequentially unique ID shared across all instances
Each student's ID resets when a new object is created
The ID is the same for all students
questions.answer: Each student gets a sequentially unique ID shared across all instances

questions.question:
The capstone Student class has three overloaded displayInfo methods:
displayInfo()
displayInfo(boolean brief)
displayInfo(String format)
Which concept from Day 30 does this directly demonstrate?
The capstone Course class has overloaded enroll methods that accept a single Student, a Student array, and an ArrayList<Student>. What is the primary benefit of this overloading?
options: It makes the code run faster
It allows callers to enroll students using whichever data structure they have
It prevents compilation errors in the main method
It is required by Java when using ArrayLists
questions.answer: It allows callers to enroll students using whichever data structure they have

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes what happens when Java cannot find an exact parameter match for an overloaded method call?
options: Java automatically widens (promotes) the argument type to find a matching method
Java converts the argument to a String and looks for a String overload
Java picks the first method defined in the source file
questions.answer: Java automatically widens (promotes) the argument type to find a matching method
